name: Oatmeal-Raisin Cookies - WW
recipeIngredient: 4 1/2 oz Uncooked quick or Old-fashioned oats
1/2 cup Dark brown sugar, packed firmly
1/2 cup Apple juice concentrate (no sugar added)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium mixing bowl, combine flour, oats, salt, baking soda, and cinnamon. Set aside.
2. Using an electric mixer in a small mixing bowl, beat together sugar, juice, oil, and vanilla. Add to oat mixture and mix well.
3. Stir in raisins and set aside.
4. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). In a separate medium mixing bowl with clean beaters, beat egg whites at low speed until foamy. Add cream of tartar and continue beating until soft peaks form.
5. Fold the beaten egg whites into the oatmeal mixture.
6. Spray a nonstick cookie sheet with non-stick cooking spray. Drop batter by heaping teaspoonfuls onto the sprayed sheet, forming 12 equal cookies and leaving about 2 inches between each.
7. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes, or until the edges of the cookies are lightly browned. Using a spatula, remove cookies to a wire rack to cool.
8. Repeat the procedure two more times, spraying the cookie sheet with nonstick cooking spray each time and making 24 more cookies in total.
Makes 12 servings, with 3 cookies each.
